:PROPERTIES:
:corpus-source:   ROBOT-report
:corpus-id:       clean-baseline
:corpus-severity: -
:corpus-url:      https://robot.obolibrary.org/report
:corpus-expects:  none
:corpus-rationale: Known-clean control for `elot_omn_report'.  ROBOT
 report should come back empty (no violations) regardless of which
 default queries it happens to run.
:END:
#+title: ROBOT report -- clean baseline

* corpus-rr-clean
:PROPERTIES:
:ID: corpus-rr-clean
:ELOT-context-type: ontology
:ELOT-context-localname: corpus-rr-clean
:ELOT-default-prefix: ex:
:header-args:omn: :tangle no
:END:
** Prefixes
:PROPERTIES:
:prefixdefs: yes
:END:
| prefix | uri                                   |
|--------+---------------------------------------|
| owl:   | http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#        |
| rdfs:  | http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema# |
| ex:    | http://example.org/corpus/rr-clean/   |

** corpus-rr-clean ontology (ex:corpus-rr-clean ex:corpus-rr-clean/0.0)
:PROPERTIES:
:resourcedefs: yes
:END:
 - owl:versionInfo :: 0.0
 - rdfs:label :: "ROBOT report clean baseline"@en
 - rdfs:comment :: A small clean ontology used to verify that the
   `elot_omn_report' integration returns an empty report for an
   ontology with no known ROBOT-report violations.

** Classes
:PROPERTIES:
:resourcedefs: yes
:END:
*** Animal (ex:Animal)
 - rdfs:label :: "Animal"@en
 - rdfs:comment :: A clean class with a label and comment.
**** Dog (ex:Dog)
 - rdfs:label :: "Dog"@en
 - rdfs:comment :: Subclass of Animal.
